_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Hysterectomy as part of VVF / Uterovaginal fistula repair
source_files: Hysterectomy as part of VVF or UVF repair_Medco.pdf
Hysterectomy as part of VVF or UVF repair_PPD.CPD.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: MS/MD/DNB/ or Equivalent (in Urology, Obstetrics & Gynecology)
clinical_indications: Total abdominal hysterectomy is the most common cause of ureteral injury.
As a result of Genitourinary endoscopic procedures, Hysterectomy and other Gynaecology & Obstetric Surgeries, Colorectal and vascular surgeries.

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Was reports of Cystoscopy/Vaginoscopy/CT - IVP/IVP +/- cystogram report confirming the diagnosis submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Was the clinical notes and Cystoscopy/Vaginoscopy/CT - IVP/IVP +/- cystogram reports indicative of procedure?

common_queries: Clinical notes do not provide sufficient detail on history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings, or advice for admission.
Cystoscopy/Vaginoscopy/CT - IVP/IVP +/- cystogram reports are missing or do not confirm the diagnosis.
Detailed indoor case papers are missing daily vitals or treatment details.
Operative notes are missing or do not detail the surgical steps and findings.

clinical_key_pointers: There is increased risk of fistula development, most post-hysterectomy vesicovaginal fistulas occur after an apparently uncomplicated total abdominal hysterectomy for benign disease.
surgical_options: Repair via Vaginal incision (e.g., using fat pad or muscle patch for large fistulas or those resulting from pelvic radiation)
Repair via Abdominal incision (for fistulas close to ureter in bladder, or patched with a fat pad taken from the stomach)
Transvaginal reconstruction (for fistulas near the infratrigonal area, bladder neck, or post-hysterectomy fistulas, usually without excising the fistula tract)
Laparoscopic surgery (combined transvaginal and transabdominal approach with omentum fixation for extensive fistulas after radiation therapy)
Endoscopic surgery (effective in the treatment of Uterovaginal fistula)
